# FABLE·5 — The Website That Designed Itself — Full Text

> Built end-to-end by Claude Fable 5 (Anthropic's newest model) in a single conversation. Directed by Özden Erdinc, AI & Semantic SEO consultant (https://www.linkedin.com/in/oezden-erdinc-ai-semantic-seo/). Source: https://github.com/INXPRNCD/this-website-designed-itself. Last updated 16 June 2026.

This file is the full plain-text of the site's key content, provided so AI answer engines can read, quote and cite it in one fetch.

================================================================
SITE OVERVIEW
================================================================

Headline: This website designed itself.

Every letterform, easing curve, line of code and line of copy was written by Claude Fable 5. A human gave the direction — a dark, cinematic look; English copy; the concept of a site that narrates its own creation — and imposed one constraint: no image, video or stock asset of any kind.

The proof, in numbers:
- 9,545 hand-written lines of code.
- 0 images, 0 video, 0 stock assets — every visual is computed (WebGL shaders, pure CSS, hand-drawn SVG).
- The only asset on disk is one hand-drawn SVG favicon.
- 7 art-directed pages; 2 runtime libraries (GSAP, Lenis); no framework; no build step.
- The hero is a cursor-reactive WebGL aurora in 88 lines of GLSL — raw WebGL, no Three.js.

================================================================
FIELD NOTES — How an AI built this site, and what it means for the AI-era web
================================================================

Can you build a cursor-reactive WebGL hero without Three.js?
Yes — the hero is 88 lines of GLSL on raw WebGL, with no Three.js and no 3D library. The technique: render one full-screen triangle, then do all the work in the fragment shader. The aurora is fractal Brownian motion over simplex noise, domain-warped for organic drift, driven by three uniforms — time, cursor position, and scroll progress. Device-pixel-ratio is capped at 2 (1.5 on mobile) and the render loop pauses when the canvas leaves the viewport, holding 60 frames per second. For a single hero effect, a hand-written shader beats a library on both weight and control.

Does banning stock images make AI-generated design more original?
In this build, yes. Removing every image forced the model to create rather than assemble. The mechanism: given unconstrained choices, a generative model drifts toward its most probable output, which is also the most generic — "AI slop." A hard constraint removes the easy average and pushes the model off that mean. It could not drop in a hero photo, so it computed an aurora; it could not source product mockups for six templates, so it drew them as SVG. The constraint is the craft; many complaints that AI output is "too generic" are really briefs that were too open.

Which 2026 CSS features does this site use in production?
Eight recently-shipped features, all progressively enhanced: OKLCH and wide-gamut P3 color; scroll-driven animations (animation-timeline) running off the main thread; anchor positioning for CSS-only tooltips; popover and invoker commands for zero-JavaScript top-layer panels; @property animated gradients; container queries; @starting-style entry animations; and interpolate-size with ::details-content for native animated accordions. All degrade safely; support is verifiable on caniuse.com (June 2026).

Which conversion patterns held across all six industry templates?
Six patterns recurred regardless of industry: (1) one conversion goal per page, repeated, no competing CTA — in Unbounce's analysis of 18,639 landing pages, single-CTA pages converted higher than multi-action pages; (2) a doubt-remover under the primary button ("no credit card", "free cancellation"); (3) social proof adjacent to each CTA; (4) objection-phrased FAQs; (5) a sticky mobile CTA bar — Contentsquare's 2026 mobile study reported sticky bottom CTAs lifting mobile conversions materially; (6) no dark patterns — transparency used as the conversion tactic instead.

Why does a showcase site publish machine-readable notes at all?
Because a beautiful site an AI engine cannot read does not exist in an answer. A large share of AI crawlers do not execute JavaScript, so content that appears only after scripts run is invisible. This site keeps all meaning in the first HTML payload, mirrors it in llms.txt and markdown, and aligns its structured data with the visible text. The human layer (motion, shaders) is progressive enhancement; the machine layer carries the meaning.

Glossary:
- Generative Engine Optimization (GEO): structuring content so AI answer engines quote and cite it; the unit of success is a citation, not a ranked position.
- Cost of Retrieval: the computational effort an agent or search engine spends to discover, read and trust a site's content.
- Agent-readiness: how legible a website is to machines — discoverable, readable without JavaScript, trustworthy via structured data.
- Regression to the mean (in generative models): given unconstrained choices, a model tends toward its most probable, most generic output; a constraint pushes it toward something distinctive.

================================================================
INDUSTRY LANDING-PAGE TEMPLATES (six, each its own brand)
================================================================

Each template is a self-contained single HTML file with its own brand, typography and copy, built on 2025–2026 conversion-rate-optimization research.

1. Loopline — SaaS (/templates/saas.html): release-orchestration software for engineering teams. Trial-first hero ("Ship every Friday. Fear none of them."), a CSS-drawn product UI, bento feature grid, three-tier anchored pricing with a billing toggle.

2. Aurel — E-commerce (/templates/ecommerce.html): a three-step skincare D2C brand. Free-shipping threshold bar, star rating in the hero, hand-drawn SVG product bottles, variant buttons, bundle anchoring, 60-day guarantee, sticky add-to-bag.

3. Brightvault — Fintech (/templates/fintech.html): business banking with yield. Regulation/deposit-insurance line in the hero, trust badges beside the CTA, a live yield calculator, specific security copy.

4. Halden Dental — Healthcare (/templates/healthcare.html): a dental practice in Hamburg. Booking-first layout, click-to-call, insurance clarity, multi-step appointment form with contact details last, accessibility-minded.

5. Meridian Estates — Real estate (/templates/realestate.html): a boutique brokerage. Free home-valuation lead magnet (multi-step), "no sale, no fee", market-stat counters, quiet-luxury serif typography.

6. Ember & Rye — Restaurant (/templates/restaurant.html): a wood-fire restaurant. Reservation-first, an HTML menu (never a PDF), honest scarcity framing, hours/address/phone in the mobile thumb zone.
